question_text: Two forces act on a box: one pulling it right at 180 N and one pulling it left at 150 N. A third force pulls at an angle. In which direction must the third force pull, and with what minimum magnitude, to bring the box into equilibrium?
correct_answer: The third force must pull to the left with a minimum magnitude of 30 N. This balances the resultant of the first two forces (180 − 150 = 30 N to the right).
explanation: The first two forces produce a resultant of 30 N to the right (180 − 150). To achieve equilibrium (resultant = 0 N), the third force must be equal and opposite to this resultant, so it must be 30 N to the left. Any direction other than directly left would require a larger magnitude force.
